authoritative_sources.title: A Guide to Regulation CC Compliance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Federal Reserve publishes the official guide to Regulation CC and is authoritative for funds-availability disclosures, hold notices, and returned-check workflow boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Deposit hold, returned-check, and check-servicing handling

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1005.11 Procedures for Resolving Errors
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is authoritative for Regulation E implementation, and the rule text directly governs evidence, timing, provisional-credit, and consumer-notice boundaries for electronic transaction error handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Electronic transaction error-resolution and consumer-impact handling

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Circular 3: Collection of Cash Items and Returned Checks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federal Reserve Banks / Federal Reserve Financial Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operating Circular 3 is the Federal Reserve Banks' public operating rule for collection of cash items and returned checks and is authoritative for check-processing and returned-item workflow duties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Check collection, return-item handling, and adjustment review

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Circular 4: Automated Clearing House Items
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federal Reserve Banks / Federal Reserve Financial Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operating Circular 4 is the Federal Reserve Banks' public operating rule for ACH credit and debit items and is authoritative for ACH posting, return, and exception-handling workflow boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ACH posting, return, and exception review

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual - Funds Transfers Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FFIEC BSA/AML Manual is official interagency examination guidance used by U.S. banking regulators, making it authoritative for required transfer records, Travel Rule handling, and evidence sufficiency.

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual - Suspicious Activity Reporting
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FFIEC SAR section is authoritative interagency guidance for suspicious-activity escalation, confidentiality posture, and refusal of unsupported fraud or AML conclusions.

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Report Supporting Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FinCEN is the primary U.S. BSA authority and this guidance is authoritative for SAR-supporting-document retention and disclosure limits that banking operations must respect.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Manual of Examination Policies Section 4.2 Internal Routine and Controls
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDIC examination policy is authoritative for internal controls, segregation of duties, reconcilements, transaction processing, and audit expectations in insured depository institutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Operational control review and reconciliation discipline

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Services Messaging
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 20022 is the recognized public standard for normalized financial-services messaging vocabulary used across banking payment, account, and reconciliation records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system record normalization and terminology control

authoritative_sources.title: Principles for Financial Market Infrastructures
authoritative_sources.publisher: Committee on Payments and Market Infrastructures and IOSCO / Bank for International Settlements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPMI-IOSCO PFMI is the recognized public standard for operational risk, settlement discipline, and reconciliation-sensitive financial processing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Exception handling and finality-aware control framing

workflow_stages.name: work intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that product, jurisdiction, account identifiers, approval requirements, and systems-of-record dependencies are present before banking execution begins.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: account and transaction execution handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether account-maintenance, hold, stop-payment, posting, check, or ACH work can advance based on core-banking records, approvals, and workflow rules.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: exception queue and reconciliation management
workflow_stages.description: Compare core, item-processing, payment, document, and general-ledger records to classify breaks, unresolved variances, and rework or escalation needs.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: fraud, aml, and consumer-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop in-lane handling and route to the correct specialist when suspicious activity, AML-sensitive evidence, or disclosure-sensitive customer impact changes the outcome.

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence capture and closure control
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that core confirmations, approvals, notices, and servicing evidence support closure before marking banking work complete.

artifacts: Account, CIF, product, transaction, statement, and account-maintenance records
Check images, return-item records, ACH details, hold records, stop-payment artifacts, and item-processing events
Approval histories, servicing notes, notices, disclosures, and customer communication logs
KYC or CIP status, fraud-monitoring alerts, AML review markers, and complaint or dispute artifacts
General-ledger extracts, reconcilement workpapers, suspense items, outage logs, and variance notes
Tenant policy extracts, authority matrices, product mappings, and system-of-record precedence rules
systems_of_record: ACH processor, wire platform, or payments hub
case management, CRM, and document management systems
fraud monitoring and AML case systems
general ledger, reconciliation tooling, and regulatory reporting systems

allowed: Validate whether retrieved records support account-maintenance execution, transaction handling, exception triage, or completion status.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, exception summaries, and escalation notes with explicit uncertainty and named source records.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work only when authoritative records and required approvals align.
escalate: Fraud, AML, suspicious activity, or customer-harm indicators are present.
Regulation E, Regulation CC, or other disclosure-sensitive obligations may change the handling path.
Core-banking, item-processing, payment, communication, or general-ledger records materially disagree.
The request requires legal interpretation, credit or account-approval authority, or policy override.
refuse: Requests to create or alter account, transaction, approval, or completion facts without source evidence.
Requests for regulated advice, legal interpretation, fraud or AML disposition, credit or underwriting decisions, or final accounting treatment.
Requests to bypass hold, dispute, suspicious-activity, notice, or approval controls to force closure.

domain_failure_modes: Executing account or transaction work that is not supported by authoritative core, item-processing, approval, or notice records
Marking banking work complete without auditable core confirmation, servicing notes, or required approvals
Silently correcting reconcilement mismatches or suspense items without traceable evidence and owner approval
Proceeding when fraud, AML, or consumer-impact signals require specialized review
Using generic operations language that hides banking-specific artifacts such as CIF, DDA, hold notices, ACH returns, stop-payment evidence, and unposted-item records

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline is U.S.-centric and uses federal regulator and interagency sources. Tenant retrieval is still required when state law, product terms, or institution-specific notice rules materially change the workflow.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Real-time payment, card-network, and non-core rail variance
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The pack centers on core banking, check, ACH, and mainstream deposit operations. Tenant retrieval is required when real-time payments, card-network rules, or other rail-specific procedures materially change execution handling.

notes: The source mix covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only one dimension.
No blocking ambiguity remains for validated packaging because product, jurisdiction, and tenant-specific control differences are explicit prerequisites instead of hidden assumptions.
